rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al VS. GCS, INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents. Plaintiff CCSF¿s motion to compel further discovery responses: the parties are directed to engage in further meet and confer efforts to try to resolve the issues raised by this motion and to report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. If the parties are not successful in either fully resolving or substantially reducing the issues raised by the motion, the court will appoint Everett Hewlett as a discovery referee. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LIN-CHUNG YANG VS. SWISSPORT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Momtion To Contest Clyde Machines, Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 2, DEFENDANT SWISSPORT USA, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest Clyde Machines, Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Swissport¿s motion to contest Clyde Machines¿ application for determination of good faith settlement is granted and the application of defendant Clyde machines¿ for a determination of good faith settlement is denied. Without a proposed valuation placed on the settlement between plaintiffs and Clyde Machines, the settlement cannot be determined to be in good faith per the good faith settlement/credits statutory scheme. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JONI L YOHANAN VS. JOHN D YOHANAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant ; Declaration Of John D. Yohanan And Elizabeth T. Erhardt In Support
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 24, DEFENDANT JOHN YOHANAN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant ; Declaration Of John D. Yohanan And Elizabeth T. Erhardt In Support - Defendant¿s Motion to Enforce Settlement is granted in part. Plaintiff is ordered to provide her liof appraisers within 14 days. If the parties do not agree on an appraiser within 14 days thereafter, Judge Hodge shall select the appraiser. If plaintiff does not provide her list within 14 days, defendant may unilerally select the appraiser. Yhe motion is moot as to the quitclaim deed. All other relief sought by the motion is either not appropriate at this time and/or is beyond the scope of the parties¿ agreements. Defendant is awarded fees and costs in the amount of $4,480 (15 hours at $30 per hour plus $40 in costs). The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW M. KING VS. FREMONT INVESTMENT & LOAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 4, DEFENDANT SIGNATURE GROUP HOLDINGS, INC., SUCCESSOR IN DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Signature Group Holdings¿ demurrer to second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to plead facts to support an application of the discovery rule or another exception to the statute of limitations to overcome the statute of limitations defense as to all causes of action. Plaintiff also failed to plead facts showing that defendants unfairly interfered with plaintiff¿s right to receive the benefits of the contract after the contact was entered. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AURORA LOAN SERVICES LLC VS. ALEKSANDER RUDAKOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 19, DEFENDANT ALEKSANDER RUDAKOV DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Aleksander Rudakov¿s demurrer is overruled. Defendant has five days to answer the complaint. The 3 day notice is not deficient. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY HUANG VS. RUN LIANG XIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 4, 2011, Line 26, DEFENDANT RUN XIE, WAN ZHOU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment - Defendants¿ motion to set aside default is granted on the condition that they pay rent of $770 and utilities of $250 to plaintiff no later than October 11, 2011.. Defendants have shown excusable neglect and mistake. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
